title: DREAM MACHINE | Creative AI News & Insight { OCT 25 -Issue 5 }
text:
Dream Machine - Creative AI News and Insights
DREAM MACHINE | Creative AI News & Insight { OCT 25 -Issue 5 }
Report this article
Pete Woodbridge
Pete Woodbridge
Published Oct 31, 2025
+ Follow
Welcome to edition 5 of
DREAM MACHINE
. Tracking news, insight and developments on AI in the Creative Industries: covering Film, Music, Games, Media, Entertainment & Advertising.
Plus news and tutorials from all the latest tools, workflows and models.
Made for creators, developers, dreamers, artists and content peeps who want to stay up to date with all of the possibilities, shifts & debates.
Created and curated by
Pete Woodbridge
Dr John O'Hare
Steve Moyler
& the
dreamlab
community.
Editors Pick
Adobe's Latest AI Announcements- Is every tool going AI?
Adobe's Latest AI enhanced announcements
Adobe’s latest announcements show how quickly our creative tools are transforming
— from static software into intelligent systems.
At Adobe MAX 2025, the company unveiled
Firefly Foundry
,
Firefly Image Model 5
, and a new
AI Assistant
built directly into
Adobe Express
. Together, they signal a shift from manual menu+click based workflows to conversational, generative, and agentic ones —
where design feels more like
directing
than
editing
.
Adobe's announcements are showing how AI is now capable of being embedded in every layer of the creative process.
You can brainstorm on
Firefly Boards
, generate imagery with
Firefly 5
, then instantly refine and publish inside Express, Premiere, Photoshop and more.
The traditional boundaries between ideation, production, and delivery are collapsing into a fluid workflow
— powered by natural language prompts and iteration, not toolbars and mouse clicks.
Whats coming? New tools in development:
Project Scene It
, which is built on Image-to-3D and 3D-to-Image technologies, it allows tagging of individual objects with reference images, preserving each object’s unique look while freely moving it in 3D space.
Adobe Project Scene It
Other AI powered tools in development include:
Project Surface Swap
that uses AI-powered texture recognition to select and swap materials seamlessly. Keeping lighting, shading and perspective intact.
Project Turn Style
lets you edit and transform 2D objects as if they were 3D.
Project Trace Erase
removes objects and their shadows, reflections, and environmental distortions, delivering natural, context-aware edits with almost no manual cleanup.
Project New Depths
makes
editing depth as easy as adjusting brightness.
Project Frame Forward
can apply changes across entire videos based on one annotated frame and a simple text prompt, which the company says will bring the precision of photo editing to video workflows.
Project Motion Map
lets you bring static vector graphics to life.
Project Sound Stager
analyses a video’s visuals, pacing, and emotional tone, it automatically generates layered soundscapes using expert sound design logic.
Project Clean Take
uses AI to correct mispronunciations, isolate voices, remove noise, and refine delivery.
Project Graph
- a node based workflow editor, very much like ComfyUI / Weavy, for editing custom workflows utilising the entire suite of adobe tools & models.
Adobe Project Graph
Project Surface Swap
Adobe Project Turnstile
'AI in everything, everywhere, all at once'
,
Adobe’s message is clear
:
'AI in everything, everywhere, all at once'
, where the next generation of creative tools won’t just enhance human ability — they’ll
collaborate
with it. Through
Firefly Foundry,
brands can train custom models on their own visual identity, ensuring control, provenance, and consistency at scale. For independent creators, that same power brings both freedom and pressure —
when
anyone can now generate studio-grade assets in seconds, the real challenge is in making them
mean
something.
This is a broader story signalling the structural shift in creative tools:
many tool stacks are increasingly shifting toward AI native systems,
particularly as the software behemoths of the past look to keep their users from turning to AI native superpowers. It's likely lots of the things we use every day will go this way, as they compete for subscribers.
And lastly
one for Halloween-
Project Light Touch
gives "spatial lighting mode" where you can interactively move a light source around within a 3D volume for relighting a photo after you have taken it.
Have a fab Halloween!
Project Light Touch - Relight Photos Instantly

Industry Insights / Interesting Stats of the Week
A focus on
Stealth, Shadow and Secret AI Users
this week, here's some stats and reports:
Surveys in 2025 have found that nearly
half (45–52%)
of U.S. employees have used AI in their jobs without telling their bosses, with
Gen Z
and tech sector workers being the most frequent secret users.
About one-third (29–33%)
pay for their own AI tools without employer knowledge.
Primary reasons for secrecy include seeking a competitive and productivity edge over peers (36%), fear of job loss (30%), and lack of clear workplace policies on AI.
Roughly 56–57% of regular
AI users admit to hiding their usage or presenting AI output as their own work
to avoid judgement or stigma, and almost half of executives do the same.
52% of workers won't admit to using AI
—even when asked directly.
